model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Iowa State, as the 2-seed, holds a significant seeding and likely talent advantage over 7-seed Kentucky, despite the lack of available analytical ratings for the Cyclones. Kentucky's 21-13 record and KenPom rank of 28 suggest a solid but not elite team, while Iowa State's higher seed implies a stronger overall profile. The Cyclones' defensive intensity and Big 12 experience should be the difference against a Kentucky team that has shown inconsistency this season.

reasoningStep.title: Kentucky vs Iowa State Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluating the matchup between 7-seed Kentucky and 2-seed Iowa State in the Midwest Region Round of 32.
reasoningStep.evidence: Kentucky has a KenPom rank of 28 and Torvik rank of 32 with a 21-13 record, featuring balanced efficiency (offensive rank 39-40, defensive rank 32). Iowa State's analytical ratings are unavailable, but as a 2-seed they are expected to be a top-10 caliber team. The Cyclones' Big 12 schedule strength and defensive reputation under T.J. Otzelberger provide advantages. Kentucky's path through the SEC tournament and first-round win shows resilience, but the seeding disparity and Iowa State's likely superior metrics make them the favorite.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

Michael Hickey / Getty Images By Jim Root March 17, 2026 Updated March 18, 2026 6:32 pm EDT Share full article 3 The Athletic has live coverage of the First Four from 2026 Men’s March Madness. Roster attrition is a factor during the postseason in any sport. Optimistically, college basketball’s shorter regular season should mean the title picture is less altered by injuries than, say, the NBA. That is not necessarily the case this season, though. The injury bug has recently bitten two No. 1 seeds (Duke and Michigan), and a third (Arizona) played through a flurry of nagging injuries late in the season. Multiple All-Americans on dark-horse title contenders are out for the season. Advertisement Below is a repository of all the biggest injuries impacting the men’s NCAA Tournament. We have split them into two categories: Injuries worth monitoring (the players could or will return at some point during the tournament) and out for the season. The highly talented Brown missed the Cardinals’ final four games with the injury, which also kept him out of eight contests in December and January. “I’ve been working out, been doing a lot of physical stuff, you know, bumping, guarding, sprints up and down the floor.
